title: Inference, Annotations, Literals, and Widening · Type Forensics
summary: Model service categories, thresholds, identifiers, and configuration without widening away domain meaning. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a literal-safe service configuration model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict best-common, contextual, return, and evolving inference from the surrounding expression and use site.
Add annotations at public, recursive, overloaded, and external boundaries without redundantly annotating obvious locals.
Control literal widening with const bindings, readonly context, const assertions, and const type parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Primitive Wrapper Types evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Primitive Wrapper Types assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.
